message.content:
Test file: src/claim-office.spec.ts
Implementation file: src/claim-office.ts
Passing tests: 20
Recent Green phase: Added Policy interface + tracking via Map<stepIndex, Policy>. processClaim sums (damage - DEDUCTIBLE) for each damage, decrements remainingCap.

Note: upcoming tests will exercise: enchantment >= 8 → 50% reimbursement clause, dragon material → full reimbursement clause, two-sword policy (multiple items same type), cap exhaustion (payout capped at remainingCap), payout rounding down, plus error cases.

Refactor the implementation while keeping all tests green. Run autonomously, return after completion.

Working directory: /home/experimenter/experiments/runs/2026-05-27_03-39-46_claim-office-example-mapping_v6.4-metric-driven-refactor_opus-4-7-no-thinking

attachment.content:
- red: TDD Red Phase
- green: TDD Green Phase
- test-list: TDD Test List Phase
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo, defaults to 10m) - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.text: I'll start by examining the current state of the implementation and tests.
Now let me run ESLint and tests to establish the baseline.

input.command: pnpm exec eslint src/ --format json 2>/dev/null | head -200
input.description: Run ESLint to get pre-refactor smell measurements
Run tests to confirm green state

content.text:
PRE measurements:

**ESLint smells (PRE)**:
- 1 error: `max-params` on `processQuote` (5 params, max 4) at line 176

**APP Mass / McCabe per function (PRE)** — focus on key targets:

`processQuote` (lines 170-182):
- Constants: 1 (the spread object)
- Bindings: 6 (step, customer, quoteIndex, policies, stepIndex, items twice via step.items)
- Invocations: ~5 (policies.set, insuranceSum, CAP_MULTIPLIER mul, quotePremium)
- McCabe: 1

`processClaim` (lines 184-195):
- McCabe: 1 (one reduce, no branches)
- APP mass: ~28

`processScenario` (lines 197-213):
- McCabe: 2 (one if branch)
- Bindings: 4 (quoteCount, policies, results, step, index)
- Assignments: 2 (quoteCount++ counts as assignment, quoteCount=0 init)
- APP mass: ~35

The worst smell is the `max-params` violation in `processQuote`. This is the primary target.

**Naming Evaluation**:
- `processQuote`/`processClaim`/`processScenario` - clear intent
- `policies` map keyed by `stepIndex` - that's the policy number reference, fine
- `Policy.items` and `Policy.remainingCap` - clear

**Refactoring Decision**: Fix the `max-params` violation by passing a context object. The `processQuote` function takes 5 separate parameters when several relate to "scenario state". I can bundle `quoteIndex`, `policies`, and `stepIndex` into a context object — or restructure to remove the smell.

Actually, looking more carefully, the cleanest refactoring is to inline `processQuote` and `processClaim` into `processScenario` or to bundle related parameters. Given upcoming tests will exercise more claim logic (enchantment clauses, dragon material, cap exhaustion), the cleanest path is to introduce a `ScenarioState` parameter object.

Let me apply that refactor.

content.text:
Tests still pass and ESLint is clean. The reminder about TaskCreate doesn't apply here — this is a single focused refactoring task, not a multi-step workflow.

Now let me compute POST APP mass and McCabe for the refactored functions to document the deltas.

**POST measurements**:

`processQuote` (new):
- Bindings: 3 (step, state, items references)
- Invocations: 4 (set, insuranceSum, ×, quotePremium)
- Constants: 0
- McCabe: 1
- Params: 2 (was 5)

`processClaim` (new):
- McCabe: 1 (unchanged)
- Params: 2 (was 2)

`processStep` (new helper):
- McCabe: 2 (one ternary branch)
- Params: 2

`processScenario` (new):
- Bindings: 5 (policies, quoteIndex, results, state, result)
- Conditionals: 1 (if step.op==="quote")
- Loops: 1 (map)
- Assignments: 2 (let quoteIndex=0, quoteIndex++)
- McCabe: 2 (one `if`)

**Deltas**:

| Metric | PRE | POST |
|---|---|---|
| ESLint smells (file total) | 1 (max-params) | 0 |
| `processQuote` params | 5 | 2 |
| `processQuote` McCabe | 1 | 1 |
| `processScenario` McCabe | 2 | 2 |
| Tests | 20 passing | 20 passing |

The branching responsibility was extracted to a tiny `processStep` dispatcher (McCabe 2), and `processQuote`/`processClaim` now both receive a uniform `ScenarioState` context so they can be composed. This is forward-compatible with the upcoming claim tests (enchantment ≥ 8 clause, dragon material, cap exhaustion) which will need access to policy items via `state.policies` — the same parameter shape will absorb the new logic without re-introducing the parameter bloat.

Refactoring Complete:
- **Refactoring**: Introduced `ScenarioState` context object to consolidate the 5 disparate parameters of `processQuote` (step/customer/quoteIndex/policies/stepIndex) into one bundled parameter; extracted `processStep` dispatcher.
- **Smell delta**: 1 max-params error → 0 errors
- **Cognitive delta** (processQuote): n/a (under threshold both before/after)
- **APP mass delta** (processQuote): ~22 → ~17 (removed 3 parameter bindings)
- **McCabe delta**: all functions remain at floor (1 or 2)
- **Tests**: 20/20 passing

Relevant files:
- /home/experimenter/experiments/runs/2026-05-27_03-39-46_claim-office-example-mapping_v6.4-metric-driven-refactor_opus-4-7-no-thinking/src/claim-office.ts
